title: Workspace Event v1
description:
A single line in <workspace>/.lab/events.jsonl — the append-only workspace
event ledger. Per roadmap section 7.1.

This is the per-workspace event stream that the Phase 2 event-ledger
reducer will consume to compute technical_verdict, impact_demonstrated,
and confidence. Phase 1 (outcome MVP) does NOT read this stream — it
only reads outcomes.jsonl + record.json. This schema is finalized here
so Phase 2 can produce and consume events without re-specifying.

event_id.description: UUIDv4 for this event. Idempotency key for the event store.

session_id.description:
UUID of the agent session that produced this event. null when not
applicable (e.g. a human-edited event).

iteration_id.description: UUID of the iteration within the session. null when not applicable.

hypothesis_id.description:
ID of the hypothesis this event tested or evaluated. null when the
event is not hypothesis-scoped (e.g. a session-start event).

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the event was recorded.

target.description: Target host or URL the event pertained to. null when not target-specific.

action.description: Tool invocation details, when the event was triggered by a tool call.

exit.description: Exit code of the tool invocation.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the tool invocation in milliseconds.

artifacts.description: Artifacts produced or modified by this event (e.g. evidence files).

path.description: Relative path to the artifact within the workspace.

size.description: Size of the artifact in bytes.

observation.description: Free-text observation note. MUST NOT contain secrets or PII.

technical_verdict.description:
Agent's technical verdict at this event (optional; the reducer folds
these into the final technical_verdict).

reportability.description:
Agent's reportability assessment at this event (optional; the reducer
folds these into the final reportability).

confidence.description: Agent's confidence at this event, 0.0 to 1.0. Optional.

next_test.description: Suggested next test the agent should run. Optional.
examples: Test with non-existent page ID for differential response

examples.next_test: Test with non-existent page ID for differential response
